free_text: Measurement of yields and angular distributions of
gamma-quanta from the interaction of 14.1 MeV neutrons
at TANGRA (TAgged Neutrons and Gamma RAys) facility.
Deuterons of energy 80 - 100 keV on Ti tritide
in the ING-27 neutron tube at a distance of 100 mm from
the target consists of 16 (8+8) mutually perpendicular
semiconductor strips, each 6 mm wide and 55 mm long.
The overlapping strips form 64 (8x8) pixels with pixel
18 scintillation detectors based on BGO crystals
angular distributions. Resolution ~110 keV at 1.33 MeV.
HPGe crystal of 57.5 mm diameter, 66.6 mm thickness.
Background was measured in separate experiment without
For neutrons scattered on sample and surrounding.
For gamma-quanta produced in interaction of neutrons
For absorption of neutrons and gamma-quanta.
Optical model parameterizations are given in Appendix
This work was supported by RSCF (23-12-00239).

taken per 100% in the article. Divided by 100
by compiler to obtain NO-DIM units.
